title: Neural Choreography Studio
description: A dual-character animation mixing studio where the BrainStem model acts as a neural conductor controlling animation blending on the Nemetona dancer. Users assign animation clips from both models to timeline slots, adjust blend weights with coupled sliders, and use undo/redo to refine a choreographed sequence that must conserve total animation energy across all active layers.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) that implements a Neural Choreography Studio. Load both GLB models: BrainStem (left side, scale 8) and Nemetona (right side, scale 3). Use GLTFLoader and set up an AnimationMixer for each model. Place a PerspectiveCamera at (0,1.5,4) looking at origin. Add hemisphere light (sky 0x8899ff, ground 0x443322, intensity 1.5) and a directional light with shadows. Extract all animation clips from both models. Create an HTML overlay panel at the bottom with: (1) A timeline bar with 6 slots, each slot is a drop target. (2) A clip palette listing all clips from both models as draggable buttons color-coded by source (blue for BrainStem, gold for Nemetona). (3) Per-slot blend weight sliders (0-1). (4) A master energy meter showing total energy (sum of all active blend weights must equal exactly 3.0 — the conservation law). (5) Undo and Redo buttons. Users drag clips into timeline slots (steps 1-6), then adjust blend weights via sliders (steps 7-10). When a weight changes, all other active slot weights auto-redistribute to maintain the 3.0 energy total (multi-input coupling). The energy meter glows green when balanced, red when adjusting. Pressing Play crossfades through the 6 timeline slots sequentially, applying each clip to Nemetona via AnimationMixer.clipAction with the assigned weight, while BrainStem plays a synchronized idle or matching clip. Every user action (assign clip, change weight, remove clip) pushes to an undo stack storing full state snapshots. Undo restores previous state; Redo re-applies. Steps 11-12 are undo/redo operations. Show slot numbers, current clip names, and weight values in each timeline slot. Add a glowing particle line connecting BrainStem's head to Nemetona to visualize the neural link. Use OrbitControls for camera. Render loop updates both mixers with clock.getDelta().

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  totalEnergyBalance: number,         // sum of all active blend weights, must equal 3.0
  activeSlotCount: number,         // count of timeline slots with assigned clips
  brainStemClipCount: number,         // number of BrainStem clips assigned to timeline
  nemetonaClipCount: number,         // number of Nemetona clips assigned to timeline
  undoStackDepth: number,         // number of actions available to undo
  redoStackDepth: number,         // number of actions available to redo
  energyMeterGlowing: boolean,         // true when totalEnergyBalance equals 3.0
  maxBlendWeight: number,         // highest blend weight among all active slots
  averageBlendWeight: number,         // mean blend weight across active slots
  lastModifiedSlotIndex: number,         // index (0-5) of most recently adjusted slot
  coupledAdjustmentCount: number,         // total number of auto-redistributions triggered
}
